image_prompt: SVG scatter graph (no best-fit), Terminal velocity / m s⁻¹ (y, 0–25, ticks every 5, minor 1) vs Radius of snowflake / m (x, 0.000–0.012, ticks every 0.002, minor 0.0005); 10 points rising and curving concave-down from (0.001,6.5) to (0.010,20.7). Variant v1 noun: snowflake.

SVG scatter + straight best-fit line through origin, Terminal velocity squared / m² s⁻² (y, 0–1000, ticks every 200, minor 50) vs Radius of ice pellets / m (x, 0–0.025, ticks every 0.005, minor 0.001); 11 points on a line of gradient ≈39200 from (0,0) to (0.020,784). Variant v1 noun: ice pellets.

SVG multi-line graph Cost / GBP (y, 0–30, ticks every 10, minor 2) vs Time / hours (x, ticks 0/5000/15 000/25 000/35 000, minor 2500); four straight lines + legend — Halogen 18 W (green, 5.3→15.3), LED 4 W (orange, nearly flat 10→14), Fluorescent 10 W (teal, 8→26.5), Filament 25 W (magenta, steepest 1.5→27). Colours teal/orange/magenta/green, never IB red.

SVG scatter + straight best-fit line through origin, Mass of water evaporated after one hour / g (y, 0.00–10.00, ticks every 2.00, minor 0.5) vs Area of dish / cm² (x, 0–350, ticks every 50, minor 10); 7 points on a line of gradient k=0.0200 g cm⁻² from (25,0.5) to (325,6.5). Variant v1 gradient 0.0200.

SVG line graph Boiling point / °C (y, 50–100, ticks every 10, minor 2) vs Altitude / m (x, 0–7000, ticks every 1000, minor 250); points decreasing linearly along a best-fit line through (0,100) and the known (2700,91.5), with the (1600 m) point drawn clearly ABOVE the line as the anomaly (magenta ×, labelled). Reconstructed from the q6a results table. Variant v1 anomaly altitude 1600 m.

Four labelled FBD options for a falling snowflake. Reusable snowflake symbol (blue six-arm crystal) at the centre of each, arrows A (up) and B (down) drawn with markers; equal arrows = option 1 (correct, terminal velocity), short up/long down = 2, long up/short down = 3, only up = 4.
Series circuit with two-cell battery, bulb (circle with X), ammeter (A) and voltmeter (V) across bulb. Digital readouts 0.3 A and 9 V for variant v1.

topic: Circuit with variable resistor added in series

render_data.title: Variable resistor in series with the bulb
render_data.task: The variable resistor is in series with the bulb. Sweep its resistance: as R_var increases, the total resistance increases so the current (I = V / R) decreases, the bulb draws less power and gets dimmer.

topic: Mount Fuji results scene with thermometer (91.5°C at 2700 m)
image_prompt: Mount Fuji panorama (symmetric snow-capped peak, pinkish sky) with a thermometer reading 91.5°C in the foreground and a labelled altitude/temperature results panel listing 11 altitudes (0–3700 m) and the boiling points; 2700 m row blank; 1600 m row anomalous (97.0°C above the trend).

topic: Mount Fuji results data table (heading row blank, one row blank)
image_prompt: Two-column data table with placeholder 'Heading 1' / 'Heading 2'. Rows: altitudes 0–3700 m vs boiling point in °C from variant v1 dataset; the 2700 m row is intentionally blank for the student to fill from the thermometer reading.
